changelog:
Production-readiness pass: panic recovery, CI tests, spec compat fixes, dead-code purge.

Production reliability:
- Background goroutines (backgroundRefresh, backgroundStatsSaver, debounced lazy refresh) now run under safeGo / safeGoArg helpers that recover panics and log with stack trace. A panic in any background path no longer crashes the proxy — operators get a logged stack instead of a 3 a.m. page.
- New CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs `go vet`, `go build`, `go test -v -race -count=1` on every push and PR. govulncheck is run best-effort.
- README documents the backup/restore procedure for data/config.json and data/stats.db.

Anthropic / OpenAI compatibility:
- usage.cache_creation envelope is only emitted when there's actual cache creation (gated on CacheCreation5mInputTokens > 0 || CacheCreation1hInputTokens > 0). Previously a no-op cache_creation map confused some SDK consumers.
- X-Request-ID / Request-Id response headers added on every endpoint. Echoes client header when supplied; otherwise generates req_<uuid>. Required by Anthropic's docs.
- OpenAI error envelope now includes code and param fields (always nil unless we have specifics) per OpenAI error-handling docs. SDKs that branch on these fields no longer null-deref.

Dead-code purge (no behavior change):
- config.UpdateSettings (replaced by UpdateSettingsPartial in A17) — deleted.
- config.UpdateLogLevel (no callers anywhere) — deleted.
- config.Get() raw config exposer (no callers, would defeat the lock) — deleted.
- formatYearWeek + formatYearAndPaddedWeek tautology in config/apikeys.go — replaced with fmt.Sprintf at the single caller. ~17 lines gone.

Deferred (would expand scope or change protocol shape):
- WebSocket SSE-event parity (response.in_progress, output_item / content_part / output_text events on WS). Requires refactoring the SSE emitter into a shared writer interface.
- WebSocket connection draining on graceful shutdown. Net/http Shutdown does not handle hijacked connections; needs explicit tracking + close frames.
- SQLite migration framework. Premature; no schema change pending.
- stop_reason / finish_reason mapping for length / max_tokens / refusal / pause_turn / content_filter. Requires plumbing upstream Kiro stop reason through KiroStreamCallback.
- OpenAI streaming first-chunk role and stream_options.include_usage handling.

A17: review pass — security hardening + dead-code purge + UX bugs.
A16: real per-key limit enforcement + /v1/responses tracking.
A15: legacy fast-path tracking gap + datetime-local roundtrip.
A14: persistent SQLite statistics.
A13: highly customizable api keys.
A12: unify api keys.
A11: WebSocket transport.
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
